mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals weakening labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE declines while SPX rises by > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved corporate revenue prospects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which lowers earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO rebounds while SPX declines by > 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by FEDFUNDS above 4% compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS declines while SPX rises by > 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k jobs signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-payroll periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% as measured by CPIAUCSL signals central bank inaction that sustains higher nominal growth expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL declines while SPX falls by > 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate strong consumer and business confidence, which drives equity valuations higher through improved cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
